Contracting Authority: Energie Steiermark Business GmbH (awarding unit: Energie Steiermark AG), Leonhardgürtel 10, 8010 Graz
Project Description:
- Replacement of legacy Energy Trading Risk Management systems with a cloud-based standard ETRM solution (SaaS) incl. maintenance
- Standard product (no custom-only build); must have ≥3 productive installations; integration with Azure; multicloud possible
- Cover commodities: power, gas/biogas, EUAs, HKNs; spot and forward incl. gas storage products; DACH/central Europe
- Full deal life cycle (Front/Middle/Back Office), confirmations, regulatory reporting (e.g., equias eCM/eRR), reporting for accounting/controlling
- High availability (7x24 operations), state-of-the-art cybersecurity; DSGVO/Data Protection Act compliance
- Interfaces to existing PFM/FPM kept; migrate legacy data; documentation & train-the-trainer; further development, maintenance & support
- On-site work up to 20% effort; test access ≥14 calendar days in stage 2
Important Dates:
- Questions deadline: 2025-02-14 12:00 (receipt)
- Request-to-participate deadline: 2025-03-04 12:00 (receipt)
- Invitation to bid: ~end Mar 2025
- Service start: ~end Q2 2025
- Milestones: Go Live EUA/GoO: 2025-07-01; Go Live Gas: 2026-01-01; Go Live Power + overall acceptance: 2026-03-31

Scope & Requirements.Scope & Requirements:
- Delivery, implementation, go-live of a cloud-based standard ETRM; customizing/add-ons as needed
- Commodities: power, gas/biogas, EUAs, HKNs; spot & term; include gas storage products
- Processes: full deal life cycle; confirmations; regulatory reporting via standard interfaces (e.g., equias eCM/eRR or equivalent)
- Reporting: support accounting/controlling (e.g., MtM), ad-hoc queries
- Architecture/security: validated design; meet common security standards; certifications/attestations for critical infrastructure operation
- Operations: SaaS with 7x24 availability & Security Operations; SLAs; support & maintenance
- Integration: Azure cloud services; interfaces to existing PFM/FPM (must remain untouched); import legacy data
- Compliance: DSGVO/Data Protection Act; project language DE or EN
- Delivery model: standard software (≥3 productive installs); no lots; no variants/alternatives; no part offers
- On-site presence up to 20% of effort; documentation & train-the-trainer
- Test access: provide demo for ≥14 calendar days in stage 2
- Key milestones: 2025-07-01 (EUA/GoO), 2026-01-01 (Gas), 2026-03-31 (Power + acceptance)
Scope & Requirements.Contract Volume: Open-ended framework; bid price evaluated on TCO7 (7-year lifecycle) across: software, implementation services, maintenance, time & materials (as per vemap price list)
Scope & Requirements.Standards & Certifications:
- DSGVO and Austrian Data Protection Act
- Common architecture/security standards; certifications/attestations suitable for critical infrastructure
- 7x24 SaaS operations; Azure integration
Scope & Requirements.Subcontracting & Consortia:
- Subcontracting allowed for parts; entire contract or sub-subcontracting not allowed
- All subs disclosed in Form 7; changes/additions only with prior written consent; invited bidder cannot be sub of another invited bidder
- Consortia (ARGE) allowed; members jointly and severally liable; nominate empowered lead; membership changes prohibited during procedure
Scope & Requirements.Forms & e-Submission:
- Electronic only via https://e-steiermark.vemap.com; qualified e-signature; trustDesk vemap; encrypted submission
- No email/fax/paper; server time decisive; file names without special characters; no executable files
- Fill online forms; upload completed annexes

Offer Submission Documents: Form 11 – EU Sanctions Reg. Art. 5k declaration
Annex ./6 – Key person IT Project Manager reference
List of Documents: Company register excerpt (≤1 month old) for bidder/consortium members/subcontractors/patrons
Social security clearance (≤3 months old) or equivalent
Tax clearance (§229a BAO) (≤3 months old) or equivalent
IT liability insurance policy (≥EUR 2,000,000) incl. data losses and loss of profit
KSV 1870 rating 399 or better, or PD <1.15% (Basel II) equivalence
If KSV 400–499 (PD <7.11%): bank guarantee ≥EUR 500,000, irrevocable/unconditional/abstract, EEA bank ≥Baa3/BBB-
Power of attorney (if signatory not statutorily authorized)
Joint liability declaration for required subs (Annex B to Form 7)
Patronage letter (Form 8) when relying on third-party capacity
Criminal record certificates upon request (legal persons’ organs)
Register extract for associations upon request

Economic & Financial Standing.Minimum Turnover: Avg. annual net turnover (2021–2023) > EUR 1,500,000 (consolidate for groups; cumulate for consortia)

Legal & Registration.Trade/Professional Register Entry: Entry/proof of authorization at time of participation deadline; EU/EWR notifications per §373a GewO; Swiss deliveries ≤90 workdays/year
Legal & Registration.Self-declarations (GWB §§123/124 or equi: Non-exclusion per §249 BVergG; compliance with labour/social/environmental law; no conflicts of interest; Art. 5k EU 833/2014
Legal & Registration.Subcontractor Identification & Reliance: Disclose all subs (Form 7); Annex A/B for required subs; reliance via Form 8; changes only with prior consent

Technical & Professional Ability.Personnel Profiles:
- Key roles: 1 IT Project Manager; 1 Key Account Manager
- Experience: each ≥3 years (IT PM in software implementation; KAM in client support for implemented software)
- Language: German or English ≥B2 (CEFR); soft skills (communication, teamwork, problem-solving, reliability, travel readiness)
- Presence: IT Project Manager must attend and actively participate in stage-2 hearings/presentation
- No multiple assignments (no person covers multiple roles; no multiple people per one role)
Technical & Professional Ability.Headcount / Staffing:
- Average headcount (2023 & 2024): ≥10 FTE (incl. key staff; includes defined categories; if younger company, since inception)
- On-site capacity: up to 20% of effort
Technical & Professional Ability.Reference Projects:
- Key person (IT Project Manager) references for selection: 1=100 pts; 2=150 pts; 3+=200 pts; must match company reference criteria; documented in Annex ./6
- Timeframe: within last 5 years; successful production

Company Referrals:
- Minimum 2 company references (eligibility) within last 5 years; bidder (or consortium member/subcontractor) performed the work; no self/affiliated references
- Each must cover: delivery, implementation, maintenance of a standard ETRM; markets incl. at least Power DE and Gas TTF; exchange access (EEX or ICE) for ≥1 of Power/Gas; ≥1 year support & maintenance; same standard product as to be offered (or all candidate products if undecided)
- Up to 3 additional references for selection (100 pts each); extra scoring per reference features: DACH regionality; cloud instance; market coverage (Power DE, Gas TTF, EEX/ICE) for both Power & Gas; full deal life cycle (≥deal capture & confirmation); short-term/intraday trading (≥automatic trade ingestion & matching); risk mgmt (≥measurement/aggregation/reporting for market/counterparty/liquidity)
Award Criteria:
- Procedure: Negotiated, 2 stages; shortlist 9 to bid; after initial offers, shortlist to 5; may award on first offers
- Award (stage 2) – 1,000 pts total:
  - Total price (excl. VAT) – 500 pts; formula: (2 – offer/lowest) × 500; scope covers software, implementation, maintenance, T&M; TCO7 basis
  - Functional concept – 100 pts (application examples 1–3; design incl. user/authorization, adaptability, extensibility)
  - Technical concept – 100 pts (security 40; operations/7x24 SaaS 40; interfaces 20)
  - Organizational concept – 100 pts (completeness 20; project plan & resource efficiency 20; client needs & collaboration 30; knowledge transfer 20; additional services 10)
  - Presentation & Q&A – 200 pts (AE1 40: usability 20 + functionality 20; AE2 40; AE3 40; presentation/Q&A 80); IT PM attendance mandatory; points frozen for LABO
- Selection (stage 1) – 1,000 pts total:
  - Additional company references (max 3) – 300 pts (100 each)
  - Additional reference features across eligibility+selection references – 500 pts (8 features ×62.5 each; proportional if fewer references)
  - Key person IT Project Manager references – 200 pts (1=100; 2=150; 3+=200)
